o: Linkage specification for C-compatible function names

q: What is the output of this code?
extern "C" disables C++ name mangling.
extern "C" functions cannot be overloaded.
The _______ specification provides C linkage.
What is an inline variable?
o: A variable that can be defined in headers without ODR violations

q: What is the output of this code?
Inline static members can be initialized in class definition.
Inline variables were introduced in C++17.
The _______ keyword allows header-only static member definitions.
Rearrange from basic to advanced static members:
What is offsetof?
o: A macro that returns byte offset of a member within a struct

q: What is sizeof for classes?
o: Returns the size in bytes of an object including padding

q: Empty classes have size of at least 1 byte.
sizeof includes the vptr if present.
The _______ macro calculates member offset in struct.
Match the type inspection operations:
What is std::aligned_storage?
o: A type providing aligned raw storage for objects

q: What is std::aligned_union?
o: Aligned storage for any of several types

q: std::aligned_storage is deprecated in C++23.
alignas specifier can increase but not decrease alignment.
The _______ trait provides storage for placement new.
What is std::has_unique_object_representations?
o: Type trait to check if all bits contribute to value

q: What is std::is_layout_compatible?
o: C++20 trait to check if types have compatible memory layout

q: Layout-compatible types can be interconverted.
std::is_layout_compatible requires C++20.
The _______ trait checks memory layout equivalence.
What is object aliasing?
o: Multiple references or pointers to the same object

q: What is strict aliasing rule?
o: Restricting type punning via incompatible pointer types

q: Violating strict aliasing is undefined behavior.
char* can alias any type.
The _______ rule affects type-punning through pointers.
Match the C++20 class improvements:
What is a requires clause?
o: A constraint on template parameters using concepts

q: What is the output of this code?
Concepts can constrain template class parameters.
requires expressions can appear in class templates.
The _______ keyword introduces template constraints.
What is a module interface unit?
o: A file that exports module declarations and definitions

q: What is an export declaration?
o: Makes entity visible to module importers

q: Modules can export classes and their members.
Module interfaces replace header files.
The _______ keyword makes declarations available to importers.
Rearrange from traditional to modern C++ class organization:
What is std::source_location?
o: C++20 type providing source code location information

q: What is the output of this code?
source_location replaces __LINE__ and __FILE__ macros.
source_location is evaluated at call site.
The _______ function returns current source location.
Match the debugging utilities:
What is std::stacktrace?
o: C++23 type providing call stack information

q: What is a constrained auto?
Constrained auto enforces type requirements on deduction.
std::stacktrace was added in C++23.
The _______ feature provides runtime call stack info.
What is aggregate CTAD?
o: Template argument deduction for aggregate types

q: What is parenthesized aggregate initialization?
o: C++20 feature allowing () syntax for aggregate init

q: Parenthesized init works with aggregates in C++20.
Aggregate CTAD requires C++20.
The _______ initialization uses parentheses for aggregates.
Rearrange class features by C++ version:
What is std::is_constant_evaluated?
o: Checks if code is being evaluated at compile time

q: std::is_constant_evaluated was added in C++20.
The _______ function detects constexpr evaluation context.
What is a coroutine class?
o: A class with promise_type that enables coroutine behavior

q: Coroutines use co_await, co_yield, and co_return keywords.
